title: Fourier Neural Operator for Parametric Partial Differential Equations
abstract: We propose a new framework for learning the solution operator of parametric PDEs using Fourier neural operators (FNO). The FNO learns a mapping between function spaces by parameterizing the integral kernel in Fourier space. It is the first ML-based method to successfully model turbulent flows with zero-shot super-resolution.
method_summary: Introduces the Fourier Neural Operator that learns mappings between infinite-dimensional function spaces via spectral convolutions, achieving up to three orders of magnitude speedup over traditional PDE solvers.

title: Learning nonlinear operators via DeepONet based on the universal approximation theorem of operators
abstract: We propose DeepONet, a deep learning framework for learning diverse continuous nonlinear operators from data. DeepONet is based on the universal approximation theorem of operators, which states that a neural network with a single hidden layer can approximate any nonlinear continuous operator.
method_summary: Introduces DeepONet architecture with branch and trunk networks for learning nonlinear operators, demonstrating effectiveness on integrals, fractional Laplacians, and differential equations.

title: Neural operators for accelerating scientific simulations and design
abstract: Neural operators can extrapolate and predict solutions at new locations unseen during training. They can be integrated with physics and other domain constraints enforced at finer resolutions to obtain accurate solutions.
method_summary: Comprehensive review of neural operator architectures for scientific computing, covering FNO, DeepONet, and their applications in accelerating simulations and engineering design.

title: Learning nonlinear operators in latent spaces for real-time predictions of complex dynamics in physical systems
abstract: We propose an approach for learning neural operators in latent spaces, facilitating real-time predictions for highly nonlinear and multiscale systems on high-dimensional domains. Our method utilizes variational autoencoders to map high-dimensional data to a compact latent representation.
method_summary: Proposes latent space neural operators combining VAE-based dimensionality reduction with operator learning for real-time prediction of complex multiscale physical systems.

title: A Practical Introduction to Neural Operators in Scientific Computing
abstract: This focused review explores a range of neural operator architectures for approximating solutions to parametric partial differential equations, emphasizing high-level concepts and practical implementation. Beyond forward problem-solving, the review delves into applying neural operators as surrogates in Bayesian inference problems.
method_summary: Practical tutorial-style review of neural operator architectures including FNO, DeepONet, and their variants, with emphasis on implementation guidance for scientific computing applications.

title: Equilibrium Neural Operator: A Physics-Informed Neural Operator for Multiscale Simulations
abstract: We propose Equilibrium Neural Operator (EquiNO) as a complementary physics-informed PDE surrogate for predicting microscale physics and compare it with variational physics-informed approaches.
method_summary: Introduces a physics-informed neural operator specifically designed for multiscale simulations, embedding equilibrium constraints directly into the operator learning framework.

title: NOEM: Efficient and Scalable Finite Element Method Enabled by Reusable Neural Operators
abstract: NOEM leverages neural operators to simulate subdomains where a large number of finite elements would be required if FEM was used. In each subdomain, a neural operator is used to build a single element, namely a neural-operator element (NOE). NOEs are then integrated with standard finite elements to represent the entire solution domain.
method_summary: Proposes a hybrid approach replacing dense FEM subdomains with reusable neural-operator elements, dramatically reducing computational cost while maintaining accuracy through integration with standard FEM.

title: Finite Operator Learning: Bridging Neural Operators and Numerical Methods for Efficient Parametric Solution and Optimization of PDEs
abstract: We introduce a method that combines neural operators, physics-informed machine learning, and standard numerical methods for solving PDEs. The proposed approach unifies aforementioned methods and can be applied to parametric solution and optimization problems.
method_summary: Unifies neural operators with classical numerical methods and physics-informed learning into a single framework for efficient parametric PDE solution and optimization.

title: An FEA Surrogate Model with Boundary Oriented Graph Embedding Approach for Rapid Design
venue: Journal of Computational Design and Engineering
abstract: We present a Boundary Oriented Graph Embedding (BOGE) approach for the Graph Neural Network (GNN) to assist in rapid design and digital prototyping. The cantilever beam problem has been used to demonstrate the approach.
method_summary: Introduces BOGE, a graph embedding method that encodes boundary conditions into GNN-based FEA surrogates, enabling rapid structural design evaluation without full FEA computation.

title: A Comprehensive Evaluation of Graph Neural Networks and Physics Informed Learning for Surrogate Modelling of Finite Element Analysis
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive evaluation of graph neural networks (GNNs) and 3D U-Nets as surrogates for FEA of parametric I-beams. We introduce a Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN) framework for structural analysis.
method_summary: Benchmarks GNN and 3D U-Net architectures as FEA surrogates on parametric structural problems, providing systematic comparison of accuracy, generalization, and computational efficiency.

title: A Comprehensive Comparison of Neural Operators for 3D Industry-Scale Engineering Designs
abstract: Neural operators have emerged as powerful tools for learning nonlinear mappings between function spaces, enabling real-time prediction of complex dynamics in diverse scientific and engineering applications.
method_summary: Provides the first large-scale benchmark comparing neural operator architectures (FNO, DeepONet, etc.) on realistic 3D industrial engineering design problems.

title: A Graph Neural Network Surrogate for 3D Finite Element Modeling: Accelerated Full-Field Parameter Identification in Aluminum Alloy 6DR1
venue: Modelling and Simulation in Materials Science and Engineering
abstract: This study introduces a novel three-dimensional graph neural network (GNN) based finite element (FE) surrogate model designed to emulate full-field tensile simulations of aluminum alloy 6DR1.
method_summary: Develops a 3D GNN surrogate that replaces full FE simulations for material parameter identification, achieving significant speedup in full-field stress/strain prediction.

title: A Physics-Encoded Fourier Neural Operator Approach for Surrogate Modeling of Divergence-Free Stress Fields in Solids
abstract: The purpose of the current work is the development of a so-called physics-encoded Fourier neural operator (PeFNO) for surrogate modeling of the quasi-static equilibrium stress field in solids.
method_summary: Introduces PeFNO that encodes divergence-free constraints directly into the Fourier neural operator architecture for physically consistent stress field prediction in polycrystalline materials.

title: Learning Deep Implicit Fourier Neural Operators (IFNOs) with Applications to Heterogeneous Material Modeling
abstract: To model the complex responses due to material heterogeneity and defects, we develop a novel deep neural operator architecture, which we coin as the Implicit Fourier Neural Operator (IFNO).
method_summary: Extends FNO with implicit layers for modeling complex material responses in heterogeneous media, improving accuracy on problems with sharp interfaces and defects.

title: Neural Operators for Stochastic Modeling of Nonlinear Structural System Response to Natural Hazards
abstract: In this work, we employ two state-of-the-art neural operators, the deep operator network (DeepONet) and the Fourier neural operator (FNO) for the prediction of the nonlinear time history response of structures under natural hazards.
method_summary: Applies DeepONet and FNO to predict stochastic nonlinear structural responses under earthquake and wind loads, demonstrating neural operators for reliability analysis.

title: Toward Reusable Surrogate Models: Graph-Based Transfer Learning on Trusses
abstract: Surrogate models have several uses in engineering design, including speeding up design optimization, noise reduction, test measurement interpolation, gradient estimation, portability, and protection of proprietary data.
method_summary: Proposes graph-based transfer learning for reusable surrogate models across different truss topologies, enabling knowledge transfer between structural design problems.

title: Shape-Informed Surrogate Models Based on Signed Distance Function Domain Encoding
abstract: We propose a non-intrusive method to build surrogate models that approximate the solution of parameterized partial differential equations (PDEs), capable of taking into account the dependence of the solution on the shape of the domain.
method_summary: Encodes domain geometry via signed distance functions as input to neural surrogates, enabling generalization across different geometric configurations without retraining.

title: Learning Mesh-Based Simulation with Graph Networks
abstract: We introduce MeshGraphNets, a framework for learning mesh-based simulations using graph neural networks. Our model can be trained to pass messages on a mesh graph and to adapt the mesh discretization during forward simulation.
method_summary: Introduces MeshGraphNets that learn to simulate physics on unstructured meshes via message-passing GNNs, with adaptive mesh refinement capability, achieving high fidelity across fluid and structural domains.

title: Data-Driven Turbulence Modeling: A Survey
abstract: We briefly recall the origin and limitations of RANS models, and then review their shortcomings and uncertainties. Then, we provide an introduction to data-driven approaches to RANS turbulence closure modeling.
method_summary: Comprehensive survey of data-driven approaches to turbulence modeling, covering neural network-based RANS closures, symbolic regression methods, and hybrid physics-ML strategies.

title: Turbulence Closure Modeling with Machine Learning Approaches: A Review
abstract: To do full justice to the potential of data-driven approaches in turbulence modeling, this article seeks to identify the foundational physics challenges underlying turbulence closure modeling and assess how machine learning can address them.
method_summary: Reviews the intersection of machine learning and turbulence closure modeling, identifying key physics challenges and evaluating how deep learning, symbolic regression, and hybrid methods address RANS limitations.

title: A Forward Look on AI Foundation Models in Computational Fluid Dynamics
abstract: Driven by the advancement of GPUs and AI, the field of Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) is undergoing significant transformations. This paper bridges the gap between the machine learning and CFD communities.
method_summary: Proposes a vision for AI foundation models in CFD, discussing how large-scale pretrained models can transform fluid simulation through transfer learning and multi-task capabilities.

title: Accelerating Transient CFD through Machine Learning-Based Flow Initialization
abstract: We present a novel machine learning-based initialization method that reduces the cost of subsequent transient CFD solves substantially, achieving a 50% reduction in time-to-convergence compared to traditional initialization.
method_summary: Uses ML models to generate intelligent initial conditions for transient CFD simulations, cutting convergence time in half by starting closer to the physical solution.

title: Physics-Constrained DeepONet for Surrogate CFD Models
abstract: This methodology is exemplified through surrogate modeling of fluid dynamics over a curved backward-facing step, a benchmark problem in computational fluid dynamics. The model was trained on computational data.
method_summary: Extends DeepONet with physics constraints (conservation laws) for CFD surrogate modeling, demonstrating improved accuracy on benchmark flow problems compared to unconstrained variants.

title: Neural Fields for Rapid Aircraft Aerodynamics Simulations
abstract: This paper presents a methodology to learn surrogate models of steady state fluid dynamics simulations on meshed domains, based on Implicit Neural Representations (INRs).
method_summary: Applies implicit neural representations as continuous surrogate models for aircraft aerodynamics, enabling rapid evaluation of flow fields at arbitrary spatial resolutions.

title: Physics-Based Simulations with Masked Graph Neural Networks
abstract: We introduce a novel masked pre-training technique for graph neural networks (GNNs) applied to computational fluid dynamics (CFD) problems. By randomly masking up to 40% of input mesh nodes during pre-training, the model learns robust representations.
method_summary: Proposes masked pre-training for GNNs in CFD, improving generalization and data efficiency by learning to reconstruct masked mesh regions during self-supervised pre-training.

title: The Neural Particle Method -- An Updated Lagrangian Physics Informed Neural Network for Computational Fluid Dynamics
abstract: It has recently been shown that instead of solving a system of equations as in standard numerical methods, a neural network can be trained solely based on initial and boundary conditions. Neural networks offer a mesh-free alternative for solving PDEs.
method_summary: Introduces a mesh-free Lagrangian PINN approach for CFD that tracks fluid particles, combining physics-informed training with particle-based discretization for free-surface flows.

title: Scalable Multi-Scale Graph Neural Networks for Physics Simulation
abstract: X-MeshGraphNet overcomes the scalability bottleneck by partitioning large graphs and incorporating halo regions that enable seamless message passing across partitions. This, combined with gradient aggregation, enables training on meshes with millions of nodes.
method_summary: Extends MeshGraphNets with multi-scale graph partitioning and halo-based message passing, enabling physics simulation on industrial-scale meshes with millions of nodes.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks: A Deep Learning Framework for Solving Forward and Inverse Problems Involving Nonlinear PDEs
abstract: We introduce physics-informed neural networks -- neural networks that are trained to solve supervised learning tasks while respecting any given laws of physics described by general nonlinear partial differential equations. We present two classes of algorithms: continuous time and discrete time models.
method_summary: Foundational PINN paper that embeds PDE residuals as soft constraints in neural network training, enabling mesh-free solution of forward and inverse problems without labeled simulation data.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Operator for Learning Partial Differential Equations
abstract: We propose physics-informed neural operators (PINO) that combine training data and physics constraints to learn the solution operator of a given family of parametric Partial Differential Equations. PINO uses the Fourier neural operator (FNO) framework.
method_summary: Combines physics-informed training with neural operator architecture (FNO), enabling operator learning with fewer data samples by incorporating PDE residual losses.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Solving Thermo-Mechanics Problems of Functionally Graded Material
abstract: This study uses PINNs to solve coupled thermo-mechanics problems of materials with functionally graded properties. An in-depth analysis of the PINN framework has been carried out by understanding the effect of network architecture and training strategies.
method_summary: Applies PINNs to coupled thermal-mechanical analysis of functionally graded materials, demonstrating mesh-free solution of multi-physics problems with spatially varying material properties.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) for Heat Transfer Problems
abstract: In PINNs, automatic differentiation is leveraged to evaluate differential operators without discretization errors, and a multitask learning problem is defined in order to simultaneously fit observed data while respecting physics constraints.
method_summary: Demonstrates PINN application to heat transfer problems including conduction, convection, and radiation, showing how physics constraints improve prediction accuracy with sparse data.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks and Extensions: A Review
abstract: In this paper, we review the new method Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) that has become the main pillar in scientific machine learning, we present recent practical extensions, and provide a structured overview of the field.
method_summary: Comprehensive review of PINN methodology and its extensions including domain decomposition, multi-fidelity, and adaptive training strategies for engineering applications.

title: Scientific Machine Learning Through Physics-Informed Neural Networks: Where We Are and What's Next
abstract: Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINN) are neural networks that encode model equations, like Partial Differential Equations (PDE), as a component of the neural network itself. PINNs are nowadays used to solve PDEs, fractional equations, integral-differential equations.
method_summary: Survey of PINN methodology covering theoretical foundations, training strategies, and applications across structural, thermal, and fluid mechanics domains.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks: From Fundamentals to Applications in Complex Systems
abstract: Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) have emerged as a versatile and widely applicable concept within the realm of artificial intelligence, impacting various science and engineering domains over the past few years.
method_summary: Reviews PINN applications in complex engineering systems including structural dynamics, thermal management, and multi-physics coupling, with focus on scalability challenges.

title: Equation Discovery, Parametric Simulation, and Optimization Using PINN for Heat Conduction
abstract: In the modeling phase, the governing equation of heat transfer by conduction is reconstructed through equation discovery using fractional-order derivatives, enabling the identification of the fractional order from data.
method_summary: Uses PINNs for simultaneous equation discovery and optimization in heat conduction problems, combining inverse problem solving with design optimization in a unified framework.

title: Sequential Deep Operator Networks (S-DeepONet) for Predicting Full-Field Solutions Under Time-Dependent Loads
abstract: Results show that once trained, the proposed DeepONets can accurately predict the final full-field solution over the entire domain and are at least two orders of magnitude faster than direct finite element simulations.
method_summary: Extends DeepONet with sequential architecture for time-dependent loading, enabling full-field structural response prediction under arbitrary load histories at 100x speedup over FEA.

title: Graph Neural Networks as Fast and High-Fidelity Emulators for Finite-Element Ice Sheet Modeling
abstract: These GNNs reproduce ice thickness and velocity with better accuracy than the classic convolutional neural network (CNN) and multi-layer perception (MLP). In particular, GNNs successfully capture the complex dynamics.
method_summary: Demonstrates GNN-based emulators for finite-element ice sheet models, achieving higher fidelity than CNN/MLP alternatives while maintaining computational efficiency for climate simulations.

title: Discovering Explicit Reynolds-Averaged Turbulence Closures Through Deep Learning-Based Symbolic Regression
abstract: This work introduces a novel data-driven framework to formulate explicit algebraic Reynolds-averaged Navier-Stokes (RANS) turbulence closures. Recent years have witnessed a blossom in applying machine learning to turbulence modeling.
method_summary: Combines deep learning with symbolic regression to discover interpretable, explicit RANS turbulence closure models from high-fidelity simulation data.

title: PhysiX: A Foundation Model for Physics Simulations
abstract: We introduce PhysiX, a family of large-scale foundation models for physics simulation. PhysiX is an autoregressive generative model composed of a discrete tokenizer, which converts heterogeneous physical processes at different scales into a sequence of discrete tokens, and employs an autoregressive next-token prediction objective to model such processes.
method_summary: 4.5B-parameter autoregressive foundation model that tokenizes heterogeneous physics simulations (fluids, solids, multi-physics) into discrete tokens and uses next-token prediction, achieving cross-domain generalization without task-specific fine-tuning.

title: OmniArch: Building Foundation Model for Scientific Computing
abstract: Foundation models have revolutionized language modeling, while whether this success is replicated in scientific computing remains unexplored. We present OmniArch, the first prototype aiming at solving diverse PDEs across different physics domains with a single unified model.
method_summary: First unified foundation model for multi-physics PDE solving, using an axis-based encoder and decoder-only Transformer to handle diverse scientific computing tasks (heat, fluid, elasticity) in a single architecture.

title: Unified Physics Transformers: A Framework For Efficiently Scaling Neural Operators
abstract: With ever increasing problem complexity, the natural question arises: what is an efficient way to scale neural operators to larger and more complex simulations. We propose Unified Physics Transformers (UPT) that encode data flexibly into a compressed latent space representation, allowing efficient operation without relying on grid or particle-based latent spaces.
method_summary: Proposes Unified Physics Transformers that compress heterogeneous physics data (grids, particles, meshes) into a unified latent space, enabling efficient scaling of neural operators to larger simulations.

title: Towards a Physics Foundation Model
abstract: Access to a Physics Foundation Model (PFM) would be transformative -- democratizing access to high-fidelity simulations, accelerating scientific discovery, and eliminating the need for specialized solvers. We propose a roadmap toward building such a model, identifying key challenges in data, architecture, and evaluation.
method_summary: Proposes a roadmap for Physics Foundation Models, analyzing architectural choices (tokenization, attention, conditioning), data requirements, and evaluation protocols for cross-domain physics simulation.

title: PINNsAgent: Automated PDE Surrogation with Large Language Models
abstract: Solving partial differential equations (PDEs) using neural methods has been a long-standing pursuit. Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) have emerged as a promising approach but require extensive manual tuning. We introduce PINNsAgent, a novel surrogation framework that leverages LLMs to automate PINN parameter tuning and architecture selection.
method_summary: Uses LLM agents to automate the configuration and tuning of PINNs for PDE solving, bridging domain-specific knowledge with deep learning through natural language interaction and automated hyperparameter search.

title: Physics-Informed Diffusion Models
abstract: We present a framework that unifies generative modeling and partial differential equation fulfillment by introducing a first-principle-based loss term that enforces generated samples to fulfill the underlying PDE constraints during the diffusion process.
method_summary: Unifies diffusion models with physics-informed constraints by embedding PDE residual losses into the denoising process, significantly improving physical consistency of generated samples for engineering simulation.
